title: Structure Editor Function Mappings
text: Structure Editor Function Mappings A keyboard translation table maps physical keyboard codes to logical Uniface structure editor functions. The keyboard codes can be found by looking in the documentation for your keyboard. Each structure editor function has a mnemonic and a corresponding numeric value 1 , which are used for defining keyboard translation tables and for the macro ProcScript statement. You can use either the mnemonic or the internal value, although mnemonics are much easier to read and remember than the codes they represent. For example, to map the keystrokes Escape S to the Uniface structure editor function ^STORE, you can use either of the following lines, enter the following line in the keyboard translation table (for a VT100-compatible keyboard): ^ESC^sS ^STORE ^ESC^sS ^127^011 Copy 1 The numeric value is the internal Uniface representation of the function, not the code from the keyboard. Related Links Structure Editor Functions A structure editor function is a request for the structure editor to perform the indicated action. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
